key_insight: Human and LLM collaboration can accelerate materials synthesis by iteratively generating and testing synthesis recipes, and it can even reveal previously undiscovered structures.
engineering_relevance: A closed-loop human-AI workflow offers a practical route to faster experimental materials discovery, especially in well-mapped but still underexplored phase spaces.
method: Generated synthesis recipes for known and candidate new Ruddlesden-Popper materials using both humans and LLMs
Tested recipe success in the lab and fed results back into a closed-loop collaboration
Compared performance across two rounds for known vs. unknown materials
evidence_signal: The abstract reports similar lab success rates for humans and LLMs, with strong performance on known materials and much lower but nonzero success on unknown materials across both rounds.
watch_item: Performance drops substantially on unknown materials, so lab validation and careful human oversight remain necessary before relying on LLM-generated synthesis plans.
paper_title: Human and LLM Collaboration for Accelerated Materials Synthesis and Discovery
